texto: My mom's like me and she doesn't want to look weak in front of other people. And she's like Colby in the way that she has to take hits at other people whenever she feels threatened. That used to make me want to cave and do what she wants. But it doesn't anymore.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a M-E Girard: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “My mom's like me and she doesn't want to look weak in front of other people. And she's like Colby…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que coragem, queer, doesn pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de queer.

frase_original: My mom's like me and she doesn't want to look weak in front of other people. And she's like Colby in the way that she has to take hits at other people whenever she feels threatened. That used to make me want to cave and do what she wants. But it doesn't anymore.

texto: It's okay to feel bad about how things went down, but it's not okay to drown in guilt and regret every day for having made decisions other people don't agree with. At some point, we all have to man up and decide to do what we have to do, despite the people around us who try to get in the way.
explicacao: Orientação direta de M-E Girard sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “It's okay to feel bad about how things went down, but it's not okay to drown in guilt and regret …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a coragem, queer, people, útil em momentos de queer.

At some point, we all have to man up and decide to do what we have to do, despite the people around us who try to get in the way.
